briefdescription.para: squared distance between two points, which is faster to compute than just distance
distance between two points, better use distanceSq for higher performance
angle in radians between two vectors

inbodydescription.para: this version is slower and less precise return std::acos( std::clamp( dot( a.normalized(), b.normalized() ), T(-1), T(1) ) );

briefdescription.para: We don't need to bind those functions in Python, because this doesn't prevent __iter__ from being generated for the type.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::constructible_from< T, U >) explicit const expr Vector2(const Vector3< U > &v) noexcept
memberdef.name: MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED

memberdef.argsstring: (!std::is_same_v< T, U >) const expr explicit Vector2(const Vector2< U > &v) noexcept
memberdef.name: MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED

detaileddescription.para: Here T == U doesn't seem to cause any issues in the C++ code, but we're still disabling it because it somehow gets emitted when generating the bindings, and looks out of place there.

inbodydescription.para: Calling sqrt this way to hopefully support boost.multiprecision numbers. Returning auto to not break on integral types.

memberdef.type: Vector2 furthestBasisVector() const MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std constexpr Vector2
memberdef.definition: Vector2 furthestBasisVector() const MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std constexpr Vector2 MR::Vector2< T >::perpendicular
memberdef.name: perpendicular

briefdescription.para: returns one of 2 basis unit vector that makes the biggest angle with the direction specified by this

detaileddescription.para: returns same length vector orthogonal to this (rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise)

briefdescription.para: NOTE: We use std::declval() in the operators below because libclang 18 in our binding generator is bugged and chokes on decltyping a.x and such. TODO fix this when we update libclang.

memberdef.definition: Vector2< decltype(std::declval< T >() - std::declval< T >())> operator-
memberdef.name: operator-
